title: The Midnight Reflection in Ashford Hall
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned antiquarian and curator of the local museum, was found dead in a dimly lit drawing‑room with a tall mirror, an ornate mantel clock, a solitary candle and a gilt‑framed photograph. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Victor Hale
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Clara Pembroke helped me shift the candle to light the victim's papers. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Dr. Alistair Finch and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Clara Pembroke and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Clara Pembroke
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, while Victor Hale shifted the candle to light the victim's papers, I moved the mirror aside. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Victor Hale and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Dr. Alistair Finch
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the clock and did not go near the mirror.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

clues.title: The Dented Clock
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the clock. Eleanor Whitcombe has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Outline beneath Candle
clues.detail: The clean outline beneath the shifted base of the candle is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Victor Hale and Clara Pembroke both state that they shifted the candle to light the victim's papers at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Clock
clues.detail: Teal-and-cream tweed fibres are caught inside a fresh split on the clock. Dr. Alistair Finch's teal tweed jacket cuff has a newly frayed cuff edge, exposing the same unusual teal-and-cream check.

clues.title: Position of Mirror
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the mirror is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack. Clara Pembroke states that they moved the mirror aside at 8:35 pm immediately before leaving the room.

clues.title: Mark on Photograph
clues.detail: A dark mark crosses the photograph. The same intact factory finish continues over the marked area.

solution.motive: Dr. Alistair Finch killed Eleanor Whitcombe because he feared exposure of his embezzlement of museum funds.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitcombe was killed by a heavy blow from the clock.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Dr. Alistair Finch claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The physical trace on the candle corroborates the action described by Victor Hale, while the trace on the mirror corroborates the action described by Clara Pembroke. They left together at 8:35 pm, and both traces are crossed by evidence from the later attack. Meanwhile, teal-and-cream tweed fibres on the clock directly contradict Dr. Alistair Finch's denial and place them at the murder object during the attack window.
